summary: Exact-match decompilation slice for GR stage files. The PR touched nine `src/melee/gr/*` stage translation units, with visible work on Flat Zone table/data separation, Home-Run Stadium pointer typing and assert macro cleanup, Pura pragma/codegen guards, and multiple stage matching cleanups using typed ground vars, local naming, stack padding, and control-flow reshaping.

key_files.role: Flat Zone matching and cleanup; separated animation lookup table use from adjacent callback table symbol, replaced raw user_data offsets with GroundVars-style accesses in shown hunks, renamed locals, and added narrow matching aids.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md#issuecomment-4745687378: removed GRFZ_ANIM_ROWS and used grFz_803E7A68 directly
raw_diff.diff: grFlatzone_802176BC, grFlatzone_802174EC, grFlatzone_80217EF0, grFlatzone_80218060, grFlatzone_802181B4

key_files.role: Home-Run Stadium matching and type cleanup; handled HSD_Text pointer storage through intptr_t boundary and restored equivalent plain HSD_ASSERT forms after review.
evidence_refs: human_pr_text.md#issuecomment-4745687378: grHomeRun_8021EC58 treated as HSD_Text* and intptr_t only at ground-var storage boundary

key_files.role: Pura matching with pragma/codegen guards; review discussed pragma scope and retained auto_inline/dont_inline behavior with validation claims.
Fourside matching improvements; visible changes include PAD_STACK, flag value change, explicit absolute-value-style branch, and temporary boolean for activity state.
Small Kongo change; review flagged an attempted __FILE__ redefine as suspicious.
Old Pupupu matching slice; changed-file metadata and bot report show matching improvements, but loaded diff details are not available in the excerpt.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl bot report: grOldPupupu_8021119C reached 100.00%; grOldPupupu_802113E0 and grOldPupupu_80210D10 improved

key_files.role: Old Yoshi matching slice; only changed-file metadata is available in the loaded evidence.
Onett matching slice; changed-file metadata and bot report show code/data matching improvements.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl bot report: grOnett_801E3A34 reached 100.00%; grOnett_801E5214, grOnett_801E43E0, grOnett_801E5538 improved; .sdata/.sdata2 improved

key_files.role: Yorster matching slice; changed-file metadata and bot report show grYorster_8020266C improved strongly.
evidence_refs: text_corpus.jsonl bot report: grYorster_8020266C improved from 86.77% to 99.39%

what_changed: PR body describes an exact-match decompilation slice for GR stage pass C, rebased onto origin/master.
Nine GR stage files changed: grflatzone, grfourside, grhomerun, grkongo, groldpupupu, groldyoshi, gronett, grpura, and gryorster.

Flat Zone follow-up removed the `GRFZ_ANIM_ROWS` offset macro approach and used `grFz_803E7A68` directly in `grFlatzone_802174EC` and `grFlatzone_80218060`, keeping the callback table and animation table separate in source.

Home-Run Stadium follow-up treated `grHomeRun_8021EC58` as returning `HSD_Text*`, using `intptr_t` only when storing through a generic ground-var slot and casting back to `HSD_Text*` before field writes.
Home-Run Stadium reviewed `HSD_ASSERTMSG(..., ptr != NULL, "ptr")` cases were reported as restored to equivalent `HSD_ASSERT(line, ptr)` forms.
Pura retained codegen pragmas in some form; comments say dont_inline behavior was kept with normalized scope and an auto_inline guard was left to preserve codegen around `grPura_80211E08`.

Visible diffs include local role renames such as `var_r29` to `trigger_machine`, `temp_r0` to `row_entry`, `temp_r4` to `anim_timer`, and `gobj2`-adjacent helper names like `sandbag_gobj`/`first_gobj`.
Visible diffs include matching tactics such as `PAD_STACK`, declaration/lifetime reshaping, local block scoping, and inline helper extraction for `grFlatzone_802181B4_inline`.
smart_moves: When review identified that an offset macro hid the real Flat Zone table issue, the follow-up removed the macro and referenced the actual table symbol directly.
For a pointer stored in generic ground-var storage, the follow-up limited integer casting to the storage boundary and recovered the typed `HSD_Text*` before dereferencing.
The author documented validation for pragma-related cleanup and final follow-up: comments cite `main.dol OK`, `dol_diff clean`, `ninja`, and clang-format passing.
Reviewer feedback was revisited across multiple equivalent assert sites instead of fixing only one line; the follow-up says all HSD_ASSERT cases were fixed.

decomp_lessons.lesson: Do not hide adjacent-table uncertainty behind offset macros. If review evidence indicates a separate table symbol, express that table directly rather than deriving it from a neighboring symbol with pointer arithmetic.
For typed pointers stored in generic integer-like union slots, keep the real pointer type at producers and consumers and use `intptr_t` only at the unavoidable storage boundary.
When `HSD_ASSERTMSG(line, ptr != NULL, "ptr")` is equivalent to an existing plain pointer assertion, prefer the simpler `HSD_ASSERT(line, ptr)` form.
Pragmas and inline-control guards may be retained only as narrow matching tactics with explicit codegen validation; they remained review-sensitive in this PR.

naming_conventions.convention: Replace `temp_rXX` or `var_rXX` locals with short role names when the local role is clear from nearby code, e.g. `trigger_machine`, `row_entry`, `anim_timer`, `spawn_left`, `item_kind`, `sandbag_gobj`, and `first_gobj`.
Keep address-style names such as `grFz_803E7A68` when semantic ownership is not fully recovered, but prefer the correct address-style symbol over deriving through a neighboring symbol.

assembly_or_matching_tactics.tactic: Use `PAD_STACK` narrowly for stack-shape matching; visible examples include `PAD_STACK(4)`, `PAD_STACK(8)`, `PAD_STACK(16)`, `PAD_STACK(0x1C)`, and `PAD_STACK(0x14)` in shown GR hunks.
Declaration locality and block scoping were adjusted to influence lifetimes and codegen, such as moving `Vec3 cam_interest`, `Vec3 pos1`, and `Vec3 pos2` into narrower scopes in `grHomeRun_8021D680`.
An inline wrapper/helper split was used for `grFlatzone_802181B4`, with `PAD_STACK(16)` in the public wrapper and the logic in `grFlatzone_802181B4_inline`.
For grPura, pragma scope and auto-inline behavior were used as matching controls, with comments claiming full PR validation after retaining or normalizing the guards.

review_feedback.feedback: Author flagged added pragmas as needing resolution; follow-up kept dont_inline behavior but normalized pragma scope and claimed `main.dol OK` plus `dol_diff clean`.
Author flagged `#pragma auto_inline off`; follow-up left the guard to preserve codegen around `grPura_80211E08` and claimed full PR validation.
Author marked a `#undef __FILE__` / `#define __FILE__ (&grKg_803E1858[0])` change as suspicious.

Reviewer objected to deriving an animation table through `grFz_803E7940` plus an offset and later said the `GRFZ_ANIM_ROWS` define only obscured the issue. Follow-up says the table is now handled directly via `grFz_803E7A68`.

Reviewer identified a pointer/integer issue and suggested `intptr_t` or fixing the return type because the value is an `HSD_Text*`. Follow-up says the function return was treated as `HSD_Text*` and integer casting was limited to generic storage.
Reviewer objected to replacing equivalent `HSD_ASSERT(line, ptr)` with `HSD_ASSERTMSG(line, ptr != NULL, "ptr")`; follow-up says all HSD_ASSERT cases were fixed.

evidence_refs: metadata_json: PR identity, state, counts, changed files
human_pr_text.md: PR body and follow-up issue comments
text_corpus.jsonl: bot match report excerpt and comments, truncated
notes: PR metadata and changed-file list are strong.
Review evidence is useful but the review_comments and raw_diff loaded files are truncated, so not every touched hunk or final post-review line is visible.
Validation claims come from PR body/comments and bot report excerpts; intake did not independently run builds, objdiff, or lint.
Some files, especially groldyoshi, groldpupupu, gronett, and gryorster, have limited loaded hunk detail; file-specific lessons for those are therefore avoided.
The PR was merged according to metadata, but this intake does not infer broader acceptance of every tactic as a standard.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Use the real adjacent table symbol instead of offsetting through a neighbor
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2723, Flat Zone review rejected deriving an animation lookup table from `grFz_803E7940` plus an offset or via `GRFZ_ANIM_ROWS`; the follow-up used `grFz_803E7A68` directly in `grFlatzone_802174EC` and `grFlatzone_80218060`, keeping adjacent tables separate in source.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Constrain pointer-to-integer casts to unavoidable storage boundaries
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2723, `grHomeRun_8021EC58` was handled as returning `HSD_Text*`; when the pointer had to pass through generic ground-var storage, the cast used `intptr_t` at that boundary and converted back to `HSD_Text*` before accessing text fields.

accepted_candidate_records.title: Prefer plain HSD_ASSERT for equivalent pointer checks
accepted_candidate_records.text: In PR #2723 review, `HSD_ASSERTMSG(line, ptr != NULL, "ptr")` was called out as equivalent to `HSD_ASSERT(line, ptr)`; the author follow-up says these were restored to plain `HSD_ASSERT`.

source_update_candidates.title: Adjacent table offsets should not be hidden behind macros when a real symbol is indicated
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding an example under literals/data ownership or typed access: if review or symbol evidence indicates a separate table starts at a nearby address, do not derive it from an adjacent table with pointer arithmetic or a macro wrapper; use the owning table symbol directly.
source_update_candidates.reason: The loaded PR provides a concrete reviewed example, but global-standard promotion should be curator-owned.

source_update_candidates.title: Pointer values in generic storage should use intptr_t only at the boundary
source_update_candidates.text: Consider adding an example to typed access/pointer-math guidance: keep a recovered pointer type at function boundaries and use sites, and use `intptr_t` only where an existing generic storage slot forces integer storage.
source_update_candidates.reason: The PR supports this as a useful reviewed pattern; curator should decide whether it belongs in global standards or path facts.

source_update_candidates.title: Flat Zone animation table symbol grFz_803E7A68
source_update_candidates.text: For `src/melee/gr/grflatzone.c`, PR #2723 follow-up states that `grFlatzone_802174EC` and `grFlatzone_80218060` should use actual animation lookup table symbol `grFz_803E7A68` directly rather than deriving it from `grFz_803E7940` or `GRFZ_ANIM_ROWS`.
source_update_candidates.reason: Path-scoped fact candidate grounded in explicit PR follow-up and review.

source_update_candidates.title: Home-Run Stadium text pointer storage boundary
source_update_candidates.text: For `src/melee/gr/grhomerun.c`, PR #2723 follow-up says `grHomeRun_8021EC58` is treated as returning `HSD_Text*`; in `grHomeRun_8021D680`, the value passes through a generic ground-var storage slot with `intptr_t` at that boundary and is converted back to `HSD_Text*` before writing text position fields.
source_update_candidates.reason: Path-scoped type/access fact candidate grounded in PR review and author follow-up.

rejection_notes: Do not generalize the retained grPura pragmas into accepted style; the evidence frames them as review-sensitive matching exceptions with validation claims.
Do not infer a global rule that `PAD_STACK` or inline wrapper extraction is preferred style; loaded evidence only shows local matching tactics.
Do not create detailed lessons for groldyoshi/groldpupupu/gronett/gryorster from this slice beyond changed-file/match-report facts because hunk evidence is limited or truncated.
Do not promote the suspicious `__FILE__` redefine in grkongo as accepted; the only loaded review comment says it smelled funky.
